source.title: Protecting Yourself and Others in Testimony Sharing
source.summary: Healthy boundaries in testimony: (1) Don't share details that could endanger you or your family (location, workplace, routines); (2) Avoid naming people who haven't given permission, especially those who might face consequences; (3) Don't glorify past sin or provide 'how-to' details that could tempt listeners; (4) Keep trauma details brief—focus on healing, not graphic descriptions; (5) Respect cultural honor/shame dynamics—consider what could bring unintended shame to your family; (6) Be wise about political/tribal sensitivities in your context; (7) Tailor your testimony to the safety level of your audience. Remember: You can be honest without being exhaustive. Wisdom isn't deception—it's discernment about what to share, when, and with whom.
